dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8200 IH: Interstate Ferry Fairness Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. LaLota (for himself and Mr. Courtney) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure

form.official-title: To amend title 23, United States Code, so that a privately or majority-privately owned ferry or ferry terminal facility is an eligible entity for purposes of participation in the Ferry Boat Program, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Interstate Ferry Fairness Act.

section.header: Amendment to allow privately owned ferries and ferry terminal facilities to be eligible for Ferry Boat Program

subsection.header: Permissibility of Federal participation in construction of privately owned ferries or ferry terminal facilities
subsection.text: Section 129(c) of title 23, United States Code, is amended—

subparagraph.text: by inserting (A) before The operation; and
by striking on a route and all that follows through the period at the end and inserting

clause.text: classified as a public road within the State and which has not been designated as a route on the Interstate System or on a public transit ferry eligible under chapter 53 of title 49; or
between 2 adjoining States and that connects one or more public roads.

subparagraph.text: Projects under this subsection may be eligible for both ferry boats carrying cars and passengers and ferry boats carrying passengers only.
in subparagraph (A), by striking shall be and all that follows through the period at the end and inserting

clause.text: majority publicly owned, if the Secretary determines with respect to such majority publicly owned ferry or ferry terminal facility that the ferry boat or ferry terminal facility provides substantial public benefits; or

with respect to a ferry that operates between 2 adjoining States or a ferry terminal facility that supports such a ferry, privately owned or majority privately owned, if the Secretary determines with respect to such ferry or ferry terminal facility that the ferry boat or ferry terminal facility provides substantial public benefits or otherwise meets the foremost needs of the surface transportation system described in section 101(b)(3)(D).
by striking Any Federal participation and inserting (i) Except as provided in clause (ii), any Federal participation; and
by adding at the end the following new clause:
Federal participation may involve the construction or purchase, for private ownership, of—

subclause.text: a ferry boat that operates between 2 adjoining States; or
a ferry terminal facility or any other eligible project under this section that supports such ferry boat.

subparagraph.text: by inserting (A) before The operating authority;
by striking such ferry and inserting a publicly owned or a majority publicly owned ferry;
by striking a privately operated toll ferry and inserting a privately operated toll ferry not subject to subparagraph (B); and
by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

A privately owned or majority privately owned ferry operating between 2 adjoining States may charge a fare for passage on such ferry in an amount not more than the sum of an amount necessary to produce revenues sufficient to cover actual and necessary costs of operation, maintenance, repair, debt service, negotiated management fees, plus an amount that the Secretary determines is a reasonable rate of return for the ferry. All revenues derived therefrom shall be applied to such actual and necessary costs, except the ferry may retain the amount that the Secretary determines is a reasonable rate of return.

paragraph.text: Section 133(b)(1)(B) of title 23, United States Code, is amended to read as follows:

subparagraph.text: ferry boats and terminal facilities that are eligible for funding under section 129(c);

paragraph.header: Construction of ferry boats and ferry terminal facilities
paragraph.text: Section 147(c) of title 23, United States Code, is amended by striking public entities and inserting entities.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall take affect with respect to a privately owned, or majority privately owned, ferry or ferry terminal facility for purposes of eligibility of the program under section 147 of title 23, United States Code, on the date that is 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act.
